commands.author: rendrag-git

target.title: fix(agents): clamp max_completion_tokens to context window for proxy-like openai-completions endpoints
target.body:
## Summary

Supersedes #85008 with a smaller patch at the canonical seam (`buildOpenAICompletionsParams`), which covers gateway, embedded runner, and public plugin-SDK consumers (`extensions/zai`, `extensions/xiaomi`, `extensions/deepseek`) in one change. Fixes #83086.

- **Problem**: OpenClaw can emit `max_completion_tokens >= model.contextWindow` on Chat Completions requests. Strict OpenAI-compatible servers (vLLM, LocalAI, llama.cpp, LM Studio, DashScope, GLM, Kimi) reject this with HTTP 400: `This model's maximum context length is N tokens. However, you requested N output tokens and your prompt contains ...`. Wildcard-discovered vLLM models in particular default to very large `maxTokens`, hitting this immediately.
- **Why it matters**: Local vLLM and other strict OpenAI-compat users see every prompt fail with HTTP 400 before the model is ever called. Issue #83086 reports the same against StepFun step-router-v1 with `context=262144`.
- **What changed**: After `resolveOpenAICompletionsMaxTokens`, clamp the resolved value to `contextWindow - 1` when `compatDetection.capabilities.usesExplicitProxyLikeEndpoint` is true and `effectiveMaxTokens >= contextWindow`. 12 lines added in `src/agents/openai-transport-stream.ts`, plus 3 unit tests.
- **What did NOT change (scope boundary)**: Native OpenAI, Azure OpenAI, OpenRouter — byte-identical defaults. The clamp is gated on proxy-like endpoint detection only. No subtracting of input tokens (deliberately — see "Why not the heuristic" below). No new helper module. No new stream wrapper. Builder seam only.

## Why a new fix instead of extending #85008

- **Canonical seam**: `buildOpenAICompletionsParams` is the single function that produces the wire payload. Fixing here covers every caller in one place.
- **#85008's interceptor at `pi-embedded-runner/extra-params.ts`** only covers traffic through the embedded runner. Gateway `openclaw infer model run` and external plugin-SDK consumers don't benefit.
- **No char→token heuristic**: #85008 uses `chars/4 * 1.2` to estimate input tokens. That's wrong by 50%+ for JSON tool schemas, Chinese, base64. Wrong-low still 400s; wrong-high needlessly truncates output. This PR instead caps the *requested output budget* to under the context window; the server still enforces `prompt + output ≤ context` itself. The misconfig case (output budget >= entire window) is what #83086 actually reports.
- **Pattern precedent**: same `usesExplicitProxyLikeEndpoint` plumbing as #71472 and the empty-tools fix in #85835. Drops into existing compat infrastructure with no new file or wrapper.
- **Diff size**: 12 LOC vs ~250 LOC across 3 files + 14 new `vi.mock()` blocks in #85008's test surface.

## Why not the full input-aware clamp (for now)

Doing it accurately requires either provider-specific tokenization or a heuristic. OpenClaw already computes `systemPromptChars + historyTextChars + promptChars` at the `[context-diag]` pre-prompt phase — a follow-up could thread that into the builder for a real input-aware clamp without a fresh estimator. That's worth doing on top of this PR but doesn't need to land together.

## Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [x] Gateway / orchestration
- [x] Integrations

## Linked Issue/PR

- Closes #83086
- Supersedes #85008 (different seam; this PR fixes the canonical builder with no heuristic estimator)
- Related #85835 (same `usesExplicitProxyLikeEndpoint` compat plumbing, same builder, empty-tools fix)
- Related #74738 (preserved `supportsModelTools` gate)
- Related #71472 (same proxy-like detection pattern)
- [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- **Behavior or issue addressed**: vLLM rejects OpenClaw OpenAI-compatible chat completions when `max_completion_tokens >= max_model_len`.
- **Real environment tested**: Local incus container `oc-stack` running OpenClaw 2026.5.18 with this patch (and the empty-tools fix from #85835) applied to the live `/usr/lib/node_modules/openclaw/dist/openai-transport-stream-*.js` bundle on the pmg gateway (port 18789). vLLM router at `http://10.68.198.1:31080/v1` serving `qwen3-5-122b-a10b-nvfp4` with `max_model_len=131072` initially, later raised to `237568` after a vLLM rollout. Model registered as `vllm/qwen3-5-122b-a10b-nvfp4`. Route taken: Discord channel `1473797645154910382` → pm agent → embedded runner → openai-completions transport.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch**: Sent ordinary Discord messages in the pm channel. With `model.maxTokens` set high (200000+) and `contextWindow=131072`, the request hits the clamp branch (`effectiveMaxTokens >= contextWindow`) and `params.max_completion_tokens` becomes `131071`.
- **Evidence after fix**: Redacted runtime logs from the pmg gateway showing the live `openclaw infer` + Discord trace:

  ```text
  [model-fetch] start provider=vllm api=openai-completions
    model=qwen3-5-122b-a10b-nvfp4 method=POST
    url=http://10.68.198.1:31080/v1/chat/completions
  [model-fetch] response provider=vllm
    model=qwen3-5-122b-a10b-nvfp4 status=200 elapsedMs=122
    contentType=text/event-stream; charset=utf-8
  embedded run agent end: runId=... isError=false
  textPreview="Hey! Testing received 👋 How can I help you today?"
  ```

- **Observed result after fix**: vLLM returned HTTP 200 with `Content-Type: text/event-stream` and the model produced a normal assistant reply. Before the patch, the same path returned HTTP 400 with `This model's maximum context length is 131072 tokens. However, you requested 131072 output tokens and your prompt contains ...`.
- **What was not tested**: StepFun step-router-v1 specifically (no account); the full input-token-aware clamp (deliberately deferred — see PR summary).
- **Before evidence**: vLLM access log returning `HTTP 400 ... max_completion_tokens=131072 cannot be greater than max_model_len=131072` on the unpatched gateway, captured during diagnostic write-up at `docs/diagnostics/vllm-openclaw-payload-compat-2026-05-23.md` (also produced in this conversation).

## Root Cause (if applicable)

- **Root cause**: `buildOpenAICompletionsParams` sets `max_completion_tokens` from `resolveOpenAICompletionsMaxTokens(model, options)` with no awareness of `model.contextWindow`. When `model.maxTokens >= model.contextWindow` (common for wildcard-discovered vLLM models, also reproducible with StepFun per #83086), the wire request asks the server for an output budget that equals or exceeds the entire context. Strict servers reject with HTTP 400; lenient servers silently truncate or accept.
- **Missing detection / guardrail**: No test exercised `model.maxTokens >= model.contextWindow` on a proxy-like endpoint.
- **Contributing context**: Wildcard discovery in `models.providers.<vllm>.models = []` populates per-model `maxTokens` from provider defaults that can match `max_model_len` and exceed conservative output budgets.

## Regression Test Plan (if applicable)

- Coverage level that should have caught this:
  - [x] Unit test
- **Target test or file**: `src/agents/openai-transport-stream.test.ts`
- **Scenarios locked in by the new tests in this PR**:
  1. Proxy-like endpoint + `maxTokens >= contextWindow` → `max_completion_tokens` clamped to `contextWindow - 1`.
  2. Proxy-like endpoint + `maxTokens < contextWindow` → `max_completion_tokens` unchanged.
  3. Native OpenAI endpoint + `maxTokens >= contextWindow` → `max_completion_tokens` unchanged (existing behavior preserved).

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

For OpenAI-compatible endpoints routed through a non-OpenAI base URL (vLLM, LocalAI, llama.cpp, LM Studio, DashScope, GLM, Kimi, and similar): outbound `max_completion_tokens` is now capped to `contextWindow - 1` when the configured `maxTokens` would otherwise meet or exceed the context window. Native OpenAI, Azure OpenAI, and OpenRouter behavior is byte-identical.

## Diagram (if applicable)

```text
Before:
maxTokens = 200000, contextWindow = 131072  -->  max_completion_tokens: 200000  -->  strict server HTTP 400

After (proxy-like endpoint only):
maxTokens = 200000, contextWindow = 131072  -->  max_completion_tokens: 131071  -->  HTTP 200
maxTokens =   8192, contextWindow = 131072  -->  max_completion_tokens:   8192  -->  unchanged
Native OpenAI: unchanged regardless of inputs.
```

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? `No`
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? `No`
- New/changed network calls? `No` (only payload field value bound on existing calls)
- Command/tool execution surface changed? `No`
- Data access scope changed? `No`

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- **OS**: Ubuntu 22.04 inside incus container `oc-stack`
- **Runtime/container**: Node 22+ / 24, OpenClaw 2026.5.18 + this patch
- **Model/provider**: vLLM serving `qwen3-5-122b-a10b-nvfp4`, `max_model_len=131072` (later raised to `237568`); OpenClaw model id `vllm/qwen3-5-122b-a10b-nvfp4`
- **Integration/channel**: Discord channel bound to the pm agent via existing routing
- **Relevant config (redacted)**: vLLM router `http://10.68.198.1:31080/v1` (Bearer token redacted); model catalog entry registers contextWindow + maxTokens explicitly.

### Steps

1. Configure a vLLM provider on a proxy-like base URL with a model entry where `maxTokens >= contextWindow`.
2. Bind a Discord channel to an agent using that model.
3. Send a message.

### Expected

- Gateway log shows `[model-fetch] response status=200`. Outbound `max_completion_tokens` is clamped to `contextWindow - 1`.

### Actual

- Matches expected.

## Evidence

- [x] Failing test/log before + passing after
- [x] Trace/log snippets

## Human Verification (required)

- **Verified scenarios**: End-to-end Discord → pm agent → embedded runner → openai-completions transport against live vLLM with `maxTokens > contextWindow`; vLLM accepts the request and the model replies normally.
- **Edge cases checked**: `maxTokens < contextWindow` is unchanged (sanity); native OpenAI byte-identical (unit test).
- **What I did not verify**: Native OpenAI live with `maxTokens > contextWindow`; StepFun step-router-v1 from #83086 (no account).

## Review Conversations

- [x] I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR.
- [x] I left unresolved only the conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment.

## Compatibility / Migration

- **Backward compatible?** `Yes` (only proxy-like detection path changes; all native providers byte-identical)
- **Config/env changes?** `No`
- **Migration needed?** `No`

## Risks and Mitigations

- **Risk**: A strict OpenAI-compatible provider expects `max_completion_tokens == contextWindow` for some reason.
  - **Mitigation**: Clamping to `contextWindow - 1` still allows up to almost the full window for output. Any prompt requires at least one token, so `output ≤ contextWindow - 1` is always a stricter upper bound than the server's own requirement.
- **Risk**: Users had set `maxTokens = contextWindow` intentionally and now see 1 less token of output.
  - **Mitigation**: This is precisely the misconfig that #83086 reports as broken. The clamp turns a guaranteed-failure into a guaranteed-success with negligible output loss.
- **Risk**: This doesn't subtract input tokens, so very long prompts can still 400.
  - **Mitigation**: Acknowledged in the PR summary; deliberate scope. Follow-up can thread OpenClaw's existing `[context-diag]` char counts into the builder for an input-aware clamp.

---

> AI-assisted PR. Investigation, full PR archaeology, patch authoring, and PR body produced with Claude (Opus 4.7). Real behavior proof is from my own local vLLM setup; I personally ran the live Discord call and inspected the outbound payload against redacted runtime logs from the pmg gateway.

- **Real environment tested:** Local OpenClaw PR checkout on host `patrick`, branch `feature/mention-pattern-policy-clean`, head `c4a2be36ad7dd3a6d544a4dab693efb978f18494`, running the real source resolver, Telegram group peer id helper, mention matcher, and inbound mention decision code through Node/tsx against redacted OpenClaw-style config/message facts. {"topic":9,"conversationId":"-1001234567893:topic:9","accountPolicyAllowIn":["-1001234567893:topic:7"],"globalProviderAllowIn":["-1001234567893:topic:global-only"],"mentionPatternsEnabled":false,"wasMentioned":false,"shouldSkip":true,"skipReason":null}

target.title: fix(tui): handle German AltGr input
target.body:
## Summary
- ignore Kitty printable key-release events in the TUI custom editor so release events cannot duplicate text
- decode German-layout AltGr Kitty CSI-u printable input when terminals report Ctrl+Alt plus a base-layout key
- add regression coverage for AltGr @/€, ordinary Alt-modified input, and printable release events

## Verification
- pnpm exec oxfmt --check --threads=1 src/tui/components/custom-editor.ts src/tui/components/custom-editor.test.ts src/tui/tui.ts src/tui/tui.test.ts CHANGELOG.md
- node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/tui/components/custom-editor.test.ts src/tui/tui.test.ts
- pnpm check:changed
- git diff --check

## Not Run
- Live German keyboard / Kitty-terminal smoke test; this environment does not have that keyboard layout attached.

## Real behavior proof

- Behavior or issue addressed: TUI input should accept German-layout AltGr Kitty CSI-u printable characters such as `@` and `€`, while ignoring printable key-release events so releases do not duplicate text. This covers issue #48897 in `src/tui/components/custom-editor.ts`.
- Real environment tested: Local Linux 6.19.14+kali-amd64 isolated worktree at PR head `6dfa6f45a0`; Node v22.22.2; pnpm 11.1.0. The smoke used a pseudo-terminal, raw stdin bytes, and the real `CustomEditor.handleInput(...)` path with `@earendil-works/pi-tui` key decoding.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch:
  1. `git switch --detach origin/main` in the isolated proof worktree, then ran a Python PTY script that spawned `node --import tsx --input-type=module`, constructed `CustomEditor`, set stdin raw, and wrote Kitty CSI-u bytes to the PTY.
  2. Repeated the same PTY/raw-stdin script at PR head `origin/pr-83947`.
  3. `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.tui.config.ts src/tui/components/custom-editor.test.ts`
- Evidence after fix: Terminal output from the PTY smoke:
  ```
  current main input bytes: ^[[64::113;7u ^[[8364::101;7u ^[[214;1u ^[[214;1:3u
  current main RESULT {"text":"ÖÖ","length":2}
  PR head input bytes:    ^[[64::113;7u ^[[8364::101;7u ^[[214;1u ^[[214;1:3u
  PR head RESULT {"text":"@€Ö","length":3}

  node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.tui.config.ts src/tui/components/custom-editor.test.ts
  ✓ tui src/tui/components/custom-editor.test.ts (5 tests) 89ms
  Test Files 1 passed (1)
  Tests 5 passed (5)
  ```
- Observed result after fix: The same raw Kitty CSI-u stream that produced `ÖÖ` on current main produced `@€Ö` on the PR branch: AltGr `@` and `€` were inserted, the ordinary `Ö` press was inserted once, and the `Ö` release event was ignored instead of duplicating the character.
- What was not tested: Did not test a physical German keyboard or the live Kitty terminal application; this environment does not have that keyboard layout attached. The smoke exercised the exact CSI-u bytes through a PTY/raw-stdin path instead.

Fixes openclaw/openclaw#48897

commands.author: Takhoffman

target.title: Disable Chrome MCP telemetry watchdog by default
target.body:
## Summary

- Pass `--no-usage-statistics` when OpenClaw launches `chrome-devtools-mcp@latest`.
- Preserve explicit profile-level `mcpArgs` overrides for usage statistics.
- Add regression coverage for the default arg list, explicit opt-in, explicit opt-out dedupe, and custom-command launches.

## Motivation / root cause

`chrome-devtools-mcp` enables usage statistics by default and can spawn a detached watchdog process. OpenClaw closes the MCP SDK stdio transport, but that only owns the direct child process. Disabling usage statistics at launch prevents the detached telemetry watchdog from being created in the default OpenClaw browser automation path.

Refs #85721

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: Browser automation launches Chrome DevTools MCP without leaving a detached usage-statistics watchdog behind after the MCP client is closed.

Real environment tested: macOS local checkout, Node 24.10.0, real `npx chrome-devtools-mcp@latest` process launched through `@modelcontextprotocol/sdk` `StdioClientTransport`.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: Started a real MCP SDK `Client` with `StdioClientTransport({ command: "npx", args: buildChromeMcpArgs({ cdpUrl: "http://127.0.0.1:9" }) })`, called `client.connect()`, `client.listTools()`, then `client.close()`, waited 1.2s, and scanned `ps axo pid=,ppid=,command=` for `chrome-devtools-mcp` and `watchdog/main.js`.

Evidence after fix: Copied live console output from the real SDK stdio close/process-scan smoke:

```json
{
  "generatedArgs": [
    "-y",
    "chrome-devtools-mcp@latest",
    "--browserUrl",
    "http://127.0.0.1:9",
    "--no-usage-statistics",
    "--experimentalStructuredContent",
    "--experimental-page-id-routing"
  ],
  "transportPid": 14467,
  "remainingRelevantProcesses": [],
  "watchdogProcessesRemaining": 0
}
```

Observed result after fix: Live output shows `--no-usage-statistics` in the generated argv, `remainingRelevantProcesses: []`, and `watchdogProcessesRemaining: 0`; no `chrome-devtools-mcp` or telemetry watchdog process remained after closing the SDK client.

What was not tested: I did not run a full Chrome browser UI workflow or a Linux remote/Testbox smoke in this pass.

## Regression tests

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/browser/src/browser/chrome-mcp.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/browser/src/browser/server-context.existing-session.test.ts extensions/browser/src/browser/server-context.stop-running-browser.test.ts extensions/browser/src/browser/routes/basic.existing-session.test.ts`

## Exact commands run

- `./node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check extensions/browser/src/browser/chrome-mcp.ts extensions/browser/src/browser/chrome-mcp.test.ts`
- `git diff --check origin/main...HEAD`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/browser/src/browser/chrome-mcp.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/browser/src/browser/server-context.existing-session.test.ts extensions/browser/src/browser/server-context.stop-running-browser.test.ts extensions/browser/src/browser/routes/basic.existing-session.test.ts`
- Real MCP SDK stdio close/process-scan smoke described above.

## User-visible behavior

Existing-session browser automation should no longer accumulate detached Chrome DevTools MCP telemetry watchdog processes during normal OpenClaw-managed MCP launches.

## Compatibility

Users who explicitly want upstream usage statistics can still pass a usage-statistics arg in `browser.profiles.*.mcpArgs`; explicit args override OpenClaw's default opt-out.

## Risks

Low. The change adds one documented Chrome DevTools MCP flag to the existing argv list and keeps profile-level custom args as separate argv entries.

target.title: feat(feishu): add delete tools for Bitable fields and records
target.body:
## Problem

The existing Bitable tools only support create/read/update operations. There are no tools for deleting fields or records, even though the underlying Lark SDK APIs are available and already used internally (e.g., in \cleanupNewBitable\).

This means the LLM cannot:
- Remove unwanted fields when repurposing an existing table
- Delete records individually or in batch
- Clean up template fields from a shared table

The LLM incorrectly reports 'the API does not support deleting fields' when in fact it's just missing the tool registration.

## Solution

Add three new tools that expose the existing Lark SDK delete APIs:

| Tool | Description |
|------|-------------|
| \ eishu_bitable_delete_record\ | Delete a single record by ID |
| \ eishu_bitable_batch_delete_records\ | Delete up to 500 records at once |
| \ eishu_bitable_delete_field\ | Delete a field by field_id (primary field protected) |

All three follow the same patterns as existing tools:
- Use \ensureLarkSuccess\ for error handling
- Support multi-account via \^GccountId\ parameter
- Return structured JSON responses

## Notes

- Primary fields cannot be deleted (enforced by the Feishu API)
- Batch delete is capped at 500 records per call (enforced by schema)
- No breaking changes to existing tools

target.title: fix(pdf): use MiniMax text models for PDF fallback
target.body:
## Summary

- Problem: MiniMax-only PDF auto-resolution could select `MiniMax-VL-01`, which is a media-understanding model and not a normal chat model for the PDF tool.
- Solution: Prefer authenticated MiniMax text chat models for PDF extraction fallback and filter MiniMax VLM refs out of PDF fallback candidates.
- What changed: PDF model config resolution now builds MiniMax text extraction candidates from the default model and configured provider catalogs.
- What did NOT change (scope boundary): Native PDF providers, image tool routing, MiniMax VLM image understanding, provider auth, and runtime secret handling are unchanged.

## Motivation

- Fixes the MiniMax-only PDF path reported in #85575, where the tool can fail before reaching text extraction fallback.

## Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix
- [ ] Feature
- [ ] Refactor required for the fix
- [ ] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [x] Skills / tool execution
- [x] Auth / tokens
- [ ] Gateway / orchestration
- [ ] Memory / storage
- [ ] Integrations
- [ ] API / contracts
- [ ] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Closes #85575
- [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior or issue addressed: MiniMax-only PDF auto-resolution chose `MiniMax-VL-01`, a media/VLM model, before PDF text extraction fallback could use `MiniMax-M2.7`.
- Real environment tested: Local OpenClaw source checkout on macOS, Node 24.8.0, using a real `MINIMAX_API_KEY` sourced from `.env` and redacted from all output.
- Before evidence: Before patch, `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/tools/pdf-tool.model-config.test.ts -- -t "does not add configured MiniMax chat models as automatic PDF image fallbacks" --reporter=verbose` passed with the old expectation that MiniMax PDF auto-resolution selected `minimax/MiniMax-VL-01` instead of the configured MiniMax chat model.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: sourced `.env` without echoing it, then invoked `createPdfTool` from the patched local source with `agents.defaults.model.primary = "minimax/MiniMax-M2.7"` against `https://www.rd.usda.gov/sites/default/files/pdf-sample_0.pdf`; also ran `git diff --check && node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/tools/pdf-tool.model-config.test.ts src/agents/tools/pdf-tool.test.ts && pnpm check:changed`.
- Evidence after fix (redacted runtime output):

```json
{
  "started": "2026-05-23T03:05:12.994Z",
  "finished": "2026-05-23T03:06:11.460Z",
  "providerCredentialSource": "MINIMAX_API_KEY present (redacted)",
  "samplePdf": "https://www.rd.usda.gov/sites/default/files/pdf-sample_0.pdf",
  "tool": "pdf",
  "model": "minimax/MiniMax-M2.7",
  "native": false,
  "attempts": [],
  "textPreview": "The PDF contains just the placeholder text \"Dummy PDF file.\""
}
```

- Evidence after fix (screenshot): captured locally as `openclaw-minimax-pdf-proof.png` for manual attachment in a PR comment.
- Additional verification: Focused PDF tool regression tests passed: 4 files, 56 tests. `pnpm check:changed` passed with lanes `core, coreTests` including typecheck, lint, import-cycle, runtime sidecar, and guard checks.
- Observed result after fix: The real PDF tool call used `minimax/MiniMax-M2.7`, ran in extraction fallback mode (`native: false`), made no fallback attempts, and correctly identified that the USDA sample PDF contains the placeholder text `Dummy PDF file.`

## Root Cause (if applicable)

- Root cause: PDF auto-resolution reused image media provider candidates for MiniMax, so it could choose `MiniMax-VL-01` even though the PDF runtime resolves candidates through the chat model registry before extraction fallback.
- Missing detection / guardrail: The prior regression test locked in the MiniMax VLM selection instead of asserting that PDF fallback should use MiniMax text chat models.
- Contributing context (if known): MiniMax chat and media-understanding models are intentionally separate; PDF fallback needs the chat model path when using extracted text.

## Regression Test Plan (if applicable)

- Coverage level that should have caught this:
  - [x] Unit test
  - [x] Seam / integration test
  - [ ] End-to-end test
  - [ ] Existing coverage already sufficient
- Target test or file: `src/agents/tools/pdf-tool.model-config.test.ts`
- Scenario the test should lock in: MiniMax configured/default text chat models are selected for PDF extraction fallback, including the `minimax-portal/MiniMax-M2.7` default-model path from the issue.
- Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail: The bug is in PDF model config selection before provider calls or PDF parsing, so unit coverage catches the broken decision without requiring live MiniMax credentials.
- Existing test that already covers this (if any): Adjacent `src/agents/tools/pdf-tool.test.ts` covers the non-native extraction fallback runtime path.

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

Users with MiniMax configured can use the PDF tool through extracted text fallback instead of failing early on a MiniMax VLM model lookup.

## Diagram (if applicable)

```text
Before:
MiniMax PDF auto-select -> MiniMax-VL-01 -> chat model registry lookup -> Unknown model

After:
MiniMax PDF auto-select -> MiniMax-M2.7 -> PDF text extraction fallback -> chat completion
```

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? No
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? No
- New/changed network calls? No
- Command/tool execution surface changed? No
- Data access scope changed? No
- Security/runtime controls unchanged: provider auth checks, model registry lookup, PDF file loading limits, SSRF policy, and runtime API-key resolution all stay on the existing paths.

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- OS: macOS
- Runtime/container: Node 24.8.0, local source checkout
- Model/provider: Real MiniMax API-key auth with `minimax/MiniMax-M2.7`
- Integration/channel: None
- Relevant config (redacted): `agents.defaults.model.primary = "minimax/MiniMax-M2.7"`; `models.providers.minimax.models = [{ id: "MiniMax-M2.7", input: ["text"] }]`

### Steps

1. Source `.env` without printing secret values.
2. Create the patched local `pdf` tool with MiniMax M2.7 as the default model.
3. Execute the tool against `https://www.rd.usda.gov/sites/default/files/pdf-sample_0.pdf`.
4. Confirm the selected model and PDF result from the redacted runtime output.

### Expected

- PDF fallback uses `MiniMax-M2.7` for extracted PDF text.

### Actual

- The real PDF tool run returned `model: minimax/MiniMax-M2.7`, `native: false`, `attempts: []`, and identified the sample PDF as containing `Dummy PDF file.`

## Evidence

- [x] Failing test/log before + passing after
- [x] Trace/log snippets
- [x] Screenshot/recording
- [ ] Perf numbers (if relevant)

## Human Verification (required)

- Verified scenarios: real MiniMax API-key PDF tool run against the USDA sample PDF, configured MiniMax API-key text model, MiniMax portal default model, existing non-native PDF extraction fallback tests, and changed core type/lint/guard checks.
- Edge cases checked: MiniMax VLM refs are filtered out of PDF fallback candidates so a fallback does not retry the old media-only model path.

## Review Conversations

- [x] I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR.
- [x] I left unresolved only the conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment.

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? Yes
- Config/env changes? No
- Migration needed? No

## Risks and Mitigations

- Risk: MiniMax PDFs that contain only scanned images still need a tested OCR/VLM PDF path.
  - Mitigation: This PR keeps scope to text extraction fallback and leaves MiniMax image/VLM behavior unchanged for the image tool.

## Out-of-scope follow-ups

- Add a dedicated MiniMax VLM PDF/OCR path only after the PDF runtime can call the media-understanding provider directly.
- Add a dedicated live MiniMax OAuth PDF lane if maintainers want recurring credentialed coverage.

target.title: fix(browser): avoid cold mac chrome version timeouts
target.body:
## Summary
- read macOS Chromium app bundle versions from `Contents/Info.plist` before spawning the browser
- extend the fallback `--version` probe timeout so cold Gatekeeper/amfid verification has room to finish
- add focused regression coverage for plist-first, fallback, and non-bundle version probes

Fixes #85418.

## Verification

2026-05-23 update after rebasing onto `origin/main` (`32f91503be`), head `a68f25d386`:

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/browser/src/browser/chrome.version.test.ts --reporter=verbose` (1 file, 3 tests passed)
- `./node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check --threads=1 CHANGELOG.md extensions/browser/src/browser/chrome.executables.ts extensions/browser/src/browser/chrome.version.test.ts`
- `./node_modules/.bin/oxlint extensions/browser/src/browser/chrome.executables.ts extensions/browser/src/browser/chrome.version.test.ts`
- `git diff --check origin/main...HEAD`

Earlier verification before the rebase:

- `OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=1 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/browser/src/browser/chrome.version.test.ts extensions/browser/src/browser/chrome.test.ts extensions/browser/src/doctor-browser.test.ts -- --reporter=verbose`
- `pnpm exec oxlint --tsconfig config/tsconfig/oxlint.extensions.json extensions/browser/src/browser/chrome.executables.ts extensions/browser/src/browser/chrome.version.test.ts extensions/browser/src/browser/chrome.test.ts extensions/browser/src/doctor-browser.test.ts`
- `git diff --check`
- `codex review --uncommitted`

## Real behavior proof
Behavior addressed: `openclaw doctor --deep` no longer has to spawn `/Applications/Google Chrome.app/Contents/MacOS/Google Chrome --version` to determine the Chrome version on macOS app-bundle installs, so a cold Gatekeeper/amfid spawn delay does not collapse into the misleading unknown-version warning.

Real environment tested: WSL Ubuntu-24.04 checkout at `/root/src/openclaw-85460`, Node/Vitest via repo wrapper.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/browser/src/browser/chrome.version.test.ts --reporter=verbose`

Evidence after fix: the regression test `reads macOS app bundle versions from Info.plist before spawning Chrome` stubs `process.platform=darwin`, calls production `readBrowserVersion('/Applications/Google Chrome.app/Contents/MacOS/Google Chrome')`, returns `148.0.7778.179`, and asserts only `/usr/libexec/PlistBuddy -c 'Print :CFBundleShortVersionString' /Applications/Google Chrome.app/Contents/Info.plist` ran with an 800 ms timeout. The fallback test proves the executable `--version` probe uses a 6000 ms timeout when plist metadata is unavailable.

Observed result after fix: focused browser version test passed 3 tests; focused static checks were clean.

What was not tested: I did not reproduce a real macOS Gatekeeper cold-cache delay in this Linux/WSL checkout; the issue report provides the live macOS timing, and this patch proves the production path avoids that spawn before falling back.

commands.author: Takhoffman

target.title: fix(codex): defer native-hook-relay unregister to avoid cleanup race
target.body:
## Problem

Gateway-dispatched Codex harness runs can unregister their native hook relay at the end of a turn while Codex hook subprocesses from the closing tool call are still calling back through `nativeHook.invoke`. When the callback lands after cleanup, OpenClaw returns `native hook relay not found`, Codex falls back to its unavailable hook response, and the `before_tool_call` chain that owns firewall, Memory Guardian, and audit enforcement can be missed for that tool call.

This is independent of #73950's module-duplication fix. A shared registry still fails if the relay ID is removed before the in-flight callback arrives.

## Change and value

This PR keeps the Codex native hook relay available for a bounded grace window after successful turn cleanup so late hook callbacks can still reach OpenClaw enforcement. The grace window has a 10 second floor and extends to the configured Codex hook timeout plus margin for longer hook subprocess timeouts.

It also makes relay handle cleanup generation-aware: if a new run reuses the same deterministic relay ID before an older deferred cleanup fires, the old handle becomes a no-op instead of deleting or renewing the new active registration.

The grace window is limited to fully successful turns. Aborted, timed-out, error-terminal, startup-failure, turn-start-failure, and Codex-reported interrupted turns unregister immediately.

## Who is affected, and who is not

Affected: Codex harness agents dispatched through the gateway path, such as `openclaw agent --agent <id>` without `--local`, when the closing tool call relies on native hook delivery.

Not affected: `--local` mode, which uses the JavaScript tool-wrapper interception path before Codex sees the tool call.

## Why now

The failure is reproducible on the current 2026.5.18 runtime stack and matches the long-running `native hook relay not found` symptom reported in #73723. ClawSweeper, the later multi-lane review, Codex review, and Claude CLI review also identified concrete stale-cleanup, aborted/interrupted-turn, and timeout-grace risks in earlier drafts; the current head addresses those findings directly.

## Implementation

- Defers normal successful end-of-run native hook relay unregister by at least 10 seconds using an unref'd timer, extended to `hookTimeoutSec + 5s` when the configured Codex hook timeout is longer.
- Keeps aborted, timed-out, prompt-error, startup, turn-start failure, and Codex-reported interrupted turns on immediate relay cleanup.
- Tracks pending deferred unregisters for deterministic test cleanup.
- Changes `NativeHookRelayRegistrationHandle.unregister()` so a handle only unregisters the exact registration it created.
- Changes `NativeHookRelayRegistrationHandle.renew()` so a stale handle cannot renew a replacement registration with the same relay ID.
- Routes expired relay cleanup through the full unregister path so bridge and permission state are removed consistently.
- Adds regression coverage for stable relay ID replacement, direct bridge replacement behavior, expired relay cleanup, aborted run cleanup, Codex raw interrupted-marker cleanup, grace-window timeout math, and quick consecutive Codex runs where the first cleanup is still pending.

## Verification

- Current PR head after conflict rebase: `b16dbcfa48ef758ff21f742134a69317ad1bd98e`.
- Rebased onto current `origin/main` `c8a35c4645dcf926d8e2c8165a1b0a2e48d24296`; `git merge-tree --write-tree origin/main HEAD` now exits cleanly.
- `git diff --check origin/main...HEAD` passed locally after the rebase.
- `./node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check --threads=1 extensions/codex/src/app-server/run-attempt.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/run-attempt.test.ts src/agents/harness/native-hook-relay.ts src/agents/harness/native-hook-relay.test.ts` passed locally.
- `timeout 120s ./node_modules/.bin/oxlint --tsconfig config/tsconfig/oxlint.core.json src/agents/harness/native-hook-relay.ts src/agents/harness/native-hook-relay.test.ts` passed locally.
- `timeout 120s ./node_modules/.bin/oxlint --tsconfig config/tsconfig/oxlint.extensions.json extensions/codex/src/app-server/run-attempt.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/run-attempt.test.ts` passed locally.
- Four-lane read-only review found additional issues after the earlier 10/10 sweep; verified findings were fixed in this PR:
  - expired relay cleanup now unregisters bridge and permission state consistently,
  - late-callback regression coverage now exercises the bridge path,
  - abort/timeout/interrupted terminal paths now fail closed instead of retaining the relay,
  - stale cleanup/renewal handles cannot mutate a replacement registration.
- `timeout 600s codex review --base ff871e162aafc0ff2e52c17b37fb4ba257601075` first found the Codex-reported interrupted-turn cleanup gap; after the fail-closed fix, rerunning the same review command reported no introduced correctness issues.
- Claude CLI review found the configured-hook-timeout grace gap; after commit `a8974c13178020074ecb37a40a29a0b7b942ac1f`, a final read-only Claude rerun on that exact pushed diff reported no actionable P0-P2 issues.
- Exact-head targeted runtime proof on rebased head `b16dbcfa48ef758ff21f742134a69317ad1bd98e` used `hookTimeoutSec=9`, confirmed cleanup grace resolves to 14s, invoked a late native `PreToolUse` callback after 11s, and observed the OpenClaw `before_tool_call` hook return a deny response.
- Local targeted Vitest attempts for the touched relay tests timed out during Vitest/Rolldown startup on the constrained WSL host before reaching assertions. No assertion failure was observed locally. The same wrapper was retried after the rebase with a 300s timeout and again timed out during startup without runner output.
- Hosted GitHub checks passed on current head `b16dbcfa48ef758ff21f742134a69317ad1bd98e`, including `Real behavior proof`, `Critical Quality (network-runtime-boundary)`, Security High shards, `check-lint`, `check-test-types`, and the relevant node/core/runtime shards.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: Codex native hook relay cleanup could run before late hook callbacks from the closing tool call reached OpenClaw, causing `before_tool_call` enforcement to be skipped. The current fix also prevents deferred cleanup or renewal from an older run from mutating a newer registration that reuses the same relay ID, aligns deferred cleanup grace with configured hook subprocess timeout, and keeps non-successful terminal paths fail-closed.

Real environment tested: Original lifecycle repro and first deferred-cleanup validation used OpenClaw `2026.5.18` stable with the local active patch set and a clean `@openai/codex@0.130.0` binary. Current PR head is `b16dbcfa48ef758ff21f742134a69317ad1bd98e` after generation-aware cleanup, abort/timeout/interrupted cleanup tightening, timeout-grace alignment, expired cleanup hardening, regression-test additions, and conflict rebase onto current `main`.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: The original live probe dispatched an `eval-2` Codex harness agent through the gateway with `openclaw agent --agent eval-2 --message "Use exec_command to run python3 -c 'print(\"x\")'" --json` while instrumenting Codex hook dispatch and OpenClaw native relay registration, unregister, and invoke lifecycle. Current-head code proof adds focused regression tests for pending cleanup, stable relay ID replacement, direct bridge replacement, expired relay cleanup, aborted run cleanup, raw interrupted-marker cleanup, and hook-timeout grace math. After the conflict rebase, I reran the targeted runtime proof against `b16dbcfa48ef758ff21f742134a69317ad1bd98e`: it used the current `resolveCodexNativeHookRelayUnregisterGraceMs(9)` value, waited past the old 10s floor, then invoked the native relay through the OpenClaw `before_tool_call` hook.

Evidence after fix: The after-fix copied runtime log below is from the instrumented OpenClaw native hook relay lifecycle trace. Before the fix, the same trace shape showed `UNREGISTER codex-08fa4130...` followed 1.2 seconds later by `INVOKE codex-08fa4130... found=false`.

```text
21:38:14.471Z UNREGISTER codex-fb6566dce... relays_size_before=0  (pre-register idempotent cleanup)
21:38:14.472Z REGISTER   codex-fb6566dce... relays_size=1
... probe runs, codex spawns relay subprocess during exec_command dispatch ...
21:38:45.684Z INVOKE     codex-fb6566dce... event=pre_tool_use found=TRUE relays_size=1
```

Observed result after fix: Late native hook callbacks can still find the relay during the normal-completion cleanup grace window. In the current code, stale cleanup and renewal handles only operate on their own registration, so a rapid second run using the same deterministic relay ID remains registered when the first run's deferred cleanup fires. Non-successful terminal paths unregister immediately so interrupted turns cannot continue accepting native hook RPCs after OpenClaw reports the run aborted.

Exact-head targeted runtime proof output:

```text
[proof] head=b16dbcfa48ef758ff21f742134a69317ad1bd98e
[proof] hookTimeoutSec=9 cleanupGraceMs=14000
[proof] relayId=fbbc4164-bacd-481c-9a1a-1c26fd34f88a
[proof] registration-after-register=true
[proof] scheduled successful-turn deferred unregister
[proof] registration-after-11s=true
[proof] before_tool_call reached event={"toolName":"exec","params":{"command":"python3 -c 'print(\"x\")'"},"runId":"run-1","toolCallId":"late-proof-call-1"}
[proof] late-invoke-exit=0
[proof] late-invoke-stdout={"hookSpecificOutput":{"hookEventName":"PreToolUse","permissionDecision":"deny","permissionDecisionReason":"proof hook blocked late native callback"}}

[proof] before-tool-call-count=1
[proof] registration-after-grace=false
```

What was not tested: A final full manual gateway-dispatched `openclaw agent --agent eval-2 ...` firewall probe has not been rerun on the rebased PR head after the follow-up hardening commits. The exact-head targeted runtime proof above exercises the timeout-grace/native-relay/before_tool_call path directly, and hosted PR checks provide the final CI-shaped validation signal.

target.title: fix(agents): treat aborted subagent runs as terminal [AI-assisted]
target.body:
## Summary

- Problem: subagent lifecycle/wait handling could treat terminal `stopReason: "aborted"` as a successful completion when the older `aborted: true` flag was absent.
- Solution: normalize the canonical aborted stop reason into a terminated error before gateway wait snapshots, run-wait normalization, registry completion, or announcement fallback can report success.
- What changed: added a shared aborted-stop-reason helper and applied it at the gateway, gateway dedupe cache, wait, subagent registry, lifecycle, and announcement-output boundaries.
- What did NOT change (scope boundary): no provider, channel, plugin API, config-shape, timeout, retry, or fallback policy changes.

## Motivation

Issue #72293 is a P1 session-state/message-loss bug. An aborted subagent run should not look successful to the parent session, because that can hide an interrupted child run during orchestration and produce an incorrect parent-facing completion state during the beta.

## Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix
- [ ] Feature
- [ ] Refactor required for the fix
- [ ] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [x] Gateway / orchestration
- [ ] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [ ] Memory / storage
- [ ] Integrations
- [ ] API / contracts
- [ ] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Closes #72293
- [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior or issue addressed: a real subagent lifecycle `end` event with `stopReason: "aborted"` is no longer converted into an `ok` parent-visible completion.
- Real environment tested: disposable Linux OpenClaw checkout, Node v22.22.2, pnpm 11.2.2.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch:

```text
pnpm exec tsx /tmp/openclaw-72293-runtime-proof.mjs .
```

- Evidence after fix (screenshot, recording, terminal capture, console output, redacted runtime log, linked artifact, or copied live output):

```text
OpenClaw aborted subagent lifecycle runtime proof
repoHead=daae39048f62
input.lifecycle.phase=end
input.lifecycle.stopReason=aborted
registry.endedReason=subagent-killed
registry.outcome.status=error
registry.outcome.error=subagent run terminated
parentAnnouncement.outcome.status=error
parentAnnouncement.outcome.error=subagent run terminated
parentAnnouncement.childRunId=proof-aborted-subagent-run
result=TERMINATED_FAILURE
```

- Observed result after fix: the same lifecycle event now becomes `endedReason=subagent-killed`, `status=error`, and the parent announcement carries `subagent run terminated`.
- What was not tested: no live external provider or channel account was used; this issue is in the local gateway/agent/subagent lifecycle path.
- Before evidence (optional but encouraged):

```text
OpenClaw aborted subagent lifecycle runtime proof
repoHead=5c4a733912a8
input.lifecycle.phase=end
input.lifecycle.stopReason=aborted
registry.endedReason=subagent-complete
registry.outcome.status=ok
registry.outcome.error=<missing>
parentAnnouncement.outcome.status=<missing>
parentAnnouncement.outcome.error=<missing>
parentAnnouncement.childRunId=<missing>
result=LEGACY_FALSE_SUCCESS
```

## Root Cause (if applicable)

- Root cause: the live wait/completion path recognized timeout-style `aborted: true`, but did not consistently treat the canonical terminal `stopReason: "aborted"` as non-success.
- Missing detection / guardrail: coverage existed for persisted killed session state, but not for raw live wait/lifecycle terminal events carrying only `stopReason: "aborted"`.
- Contributing context (if known): session lifecycle persistence already maps the same stop reason to killed, so this was a cross-layer normalization gap.

## Regression Test Plan (if applicable)

- Coverage level that should have caught this:
  - [x] Unit test
  - [x] Seam / integration test
  - [ ] End-to-end test
  - [ ] Existing coverage already sufficient
- Target test or file:
  - `src/gateway/server-methods/server-methods.test.ts`
  - `src/gateway/server-methods/agent-wait-dedupe.test.ts`
  - `src/agents/run-wait.test.ts`
  - `src/agents/subagent-registry.test.ts`
  - `src/agents/subagent-announce-output.test.ts`
  - `src/agents/pi-embedded-subscribe.handlers.lifecycle.test.ts`
- Scenario the test should lock in: `stopReason: "aborted"` cannot pass through as an `ok` wait/subagent completion, including cached gateway dedupe snapshots.

target.title: fix(install): verify node binary after auto-install and suppress ioctl errors
target.body:
## Problem

The Node.js auto-installer reports "Node.js v22 installed" without verifying the binary is actually available on PATH. On WSL2 Ubuntu 24.04, the NodeSource setup script produces ioctl errors (interactive terminal prompts in a non-interactive context), apt may fail silently, but the installer prints a success message and then immediately crashes with "Node.js v22+ is required."

Two root causes:
1. The NodeSource setup script (`bash "$tmp"`) runs without `DEBIAN_FRONTEND=noninteractive`, so it attempts interactive terminal operations that produce ioctl errors on WSL2
2. The installer prints `ui_success "Node.js v24 installed"` unconditionally after the apt call, without verifying `node` is on PATH

## Fix

1. Pass `DEBIAN_FRONTEND=noninteractive` to the NodeSource setup script execution (both root and sudo paths)
2. Run `activate_supported_node_on_path` and verify the active node meets the minimum version requirement (`node_is_at_least_required`) before printing the success message. If node is missing or too old, report the actual failure and exit instead of falsely reporting success.

Fixes #73837
## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: The Node.js auto-installer now (1) passes `DEBIAN_FRONTEND=noninteractive` via `sudo -E env` to suppress ioctl errors on WSL2 while preserving the caller's environment (proxy, PATH, etc.), and (2) verifies the active node binary meets the minimum version after package installation before reporting success.

Real environment tested: macOS (Darwin 24.5.0), Node v26.0.0, bash 5.3.9, from patched source checkout at commit 24f4134a9b.

Exact steps or command run after this patch:

Step 1. Verified patched script preserves both `-E` and `DEBIAN_FRONTEND`
```console
$ grep "sudo.*DEBIAN_FRONTEND" scripts/install.sh
        sudo env DEBIAN_FRONTEND="${DEBIAN_FRONTEND:-noninteractive}" NEEDRESTART_MODE="${NEEDRESTART_MODE:-a}" apt-get "$@"
                run_quiet_step "Configuring NodeSource repository" sudo -E env DEBIAN_FRONTEND=noninteractive bash "$tmp"
```
The `apt_get()` wrapper (line 1) uses `sudo env` with explicit vars. The NodeSource setup (line 2) uses `sudo -E env` to preserve the full caller environment while adding the noninteractive flag.

Step 2. Verified post-install guard rejects missing or too-old node
```console
$ grep -A3 "if ! node_is_at_least_required" scripts/install.sh
        if ! node_is_at_least_required; then
            local active_version
            active_version="$(node -v 2>/dev/null || echo "not found")"
            ui_error "Node.js v${NODE_MIN_VERSION}+ required but found ${active_version} after install"
```

Step 3. Exercised the guard functions from the patched `scripts/install.sh`
```console
$ bash -c '
source <(sed -n "/^NODE_MIN_MAJOR=/,/^activate_supported/p" scripts/install.sh | head -200)
source <(sed -n "/^parse_node_version_components_for_binary/,/^}/p" scripts/install.sh)
source <(sed -n "/^parse_node_version_components()/,/^}/p" scripts/install.sh)
source <(sed -n "/^node_is_at_least_required()/,/^}/p" scripts/install.sh)
NODE_MIN_MAJOR=22; NODE_MIN_MINOR=14; NODE_MIN_VERSION="22.14"

echo "=== Current node ==="
node_is_at_least_required && echo "PASS: $(node -v) >= v22.14"

echo "=== Missing node ==="
PATH="/nonexistent" node_is_at_least_required || echo "PASS: rejects missing node"

echo "=== Old node (v18) ==="
mkdir -p /tmp/fn; printf "#!/bin/bash\necho v18.0.0" > /tmp/fn/node; chmod +x /tmp/fn/node
PATH="/tmp/fn" node_is_at_least_required || echo "PASS: rejects v18.0.0"
rm -rf /tmp/fn
'
=== Current node ===
PASS: v26.0.0 >= v22.14
=== Missing node ===
PASS: rejects missing node
=== Old node (v18) ===
PASS: rejects v18.0.0
```

Evidence after fix: Terminal output from the patched install.sh confirms:

```console
$ grep "sudo -E env DEBIAN_FRONTEND" scripts/install.sh
                run_quiet_step "Configuring NodeSource repository" sudo -E env DEBIAN_FRONTEND=noninteractive bash "$tmp"
```

The `-E` flag preserves the caller's environment (proxy, PATH, locale) while `env DEBIAN_FRONTEND=noninteractive` adds the noninteractive frontend flag. The post-install guard correctly rejects missing and too-old node binaries.

Observed result after fix: All three guard conditions (pass, missing, too-old) produce the correct outcome. 24/24 install-sh tests pass including the updated assertion for the `sudo -E env` form.

What was not tested: Full end-to-end installer run through the NodeSource apt path on WSL2 (requires a clean Linux VM without Node.js). The guard logic and sudo form are verified directly from the patched script.

## Summary
- ignore Kitty printable key-release events in the TUI custom editor so release events cannot duplicate text
- decode German-layout AltGr Kitty CSI-u printable input when terminals report Ctrl+Alt plus a base-layout key

## Not Run

## Real behavior proof

- Behavior or issue addressed: TUI input should accept German-layout AltGr Kitty CSI-u printable characters such as `@` and `€`, while ignoring printable key-release events so releases do not duplicate text. This covers issue #48897 in `src/tui/components/custom-editor.ts`.
- Real environment tested: Local Linux 6.19.14+kali-amd64 isolated worktree at PR head `6dfa6f45a0`; Node v22.22.2; pnpm 11.1.0. The smoke used a pseudo-terminal, raw stdin bytes, and the real `CustomEditor.handleInput(...)` path with `@earendil-works/pi-tui` key decoding.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch:

target.title: fix(telegram): preserve forwarded context in agent body
target.body:
## Summary

- keep Telegram forwarded-message bodies clean in `BodyForAgent`
- rely on typed `ForwardedFrom*` fields for single forwarded messages so prompt assembly renders the existing structured forwarded context block
- preserve per-message forwarded attribution when Telegram forwards arrive as one debounced burst by passing typed `UntrustedStructuredContext` entries
- add regression tests for direct forwarded Telegram messages, self-forwards, mixed media/text burst coalescing, and prompt-visible forwarded context rendering

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- **Behavior or issue addressed:** Telegram forwarded messages keep the user-visible text clean for the agent while preserving forwarded attribution as typed untrusted metadata in the prompt context. This covers the direct-forward path fixed by this PR and the same prompt renderer used by forwarded batch context.
- **Real environment tested:** Local OpenClaw checkout /home/kom/proj/openclaw-pr75121 at head 2018eb88638c64162c6bffea08a0c16d12954b14 on Linux, Node $(node --version), pnpm $(pnpm --version), with the Telegram extension and auto-reply prompt code loaded from this branch.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch:**

```bash
node --import tsx --eval "const {buildTelegramMessageContextForTest}=await import('./extensions/telegram/src/bot-message-context.test-harness.ts'); const {buildInboundUserContextPrefix}=await import('./src/auto-reply/reply/inbound-meta.ts'); const c=await buildTelegramMessageContextForTest({message:{message_id:42,chat:{id:999,type:'private'},from:{id:777,first_name:'N',is_bot:false},text:'Can I borrow a drill?',date:1736380800,forward_origin:{type:'user',sender_user:{id:999,first_name:'Bob',last_name:'Smith',username:'bobsmith',is_bot:false},date:500}}}); console.log(JSON.stringify({bodyForAgent:c?.ctxPayload.BodyForAgent,raw:c?.ctxPayload.RawBody,forwardedFrom:c?.ctxPayload.ForwardedFrom,forwardedType:c?.ctxPayload.ForwardedFromType,forwardedDate:c?.ctxPayload.ForwardedDate,prefix:buildInboundUserContextPrefix(c?.ctxPayload ?? {})},null,2));"
```

- **Evidence after fix:** Terminal output copied from the command above:

```json
{
  "bodyForAgent": "Can I borrow a drill?",
  "raw": "Can I borrow a drill?",
  "forwardedFrom": "Bob Smith (@bobsmith)",
  "forwardedType": "user",
  "forwardedDate": 500000,
  "prefix": "Conversation info (untrusted metadata):\n```json\n{\n  \"chat_id\": \"telegram:999\",\n  \"message_id\": \"42\",\n  \"sender_id\": \"777\",\n  \"sender\": \"N\",\n  \"timestamp\": \"Thu 2025-01-09 04:00 GMT+4\",\n  \"inbound_event_kind\": \"user_request\",\n  \"has_forwarded_context\": true\n}\n```\n\nSender (untrusted metadata):\n```json\n{\n  \"label\": \"N (777)\",\n  \"id\": \"777\",\n  \"name\": \"N\"\n}\n```\n\nForwarded message context (untrusted metadata):\n```json\n{\n  \"from\": \"Bob Smith (@bobsmith)\",\n  \"type\": \"user\",\n  \"username\": \"bobsmith\",\n  \"title\": \"Bob Smith\",\n  \"date_ms\": 500000\n}\n```"
}
```

- **Observed result after fix:** The live output shows the forwarded text remains exactly `Can I borrow a drill?` in `BodyForAgent`/`RawBody`; the agent-visible prefix separately contains `Forwarded message context (untrusted metadata)` with the original sender, username, type, title, and forwarded date. There is no plaintext `[Forwarded from ...]` envelope in `BodyForAgent`.
- **What was not tested:** No known gaps for the local OpenClaw context-builder and prompt-renderer proof; a real Telegram Desktop/Mantis account capture was not exercised in this local run.

## Why

Telegram already parses `forward_origin` and populates `ForwardedFrom*` fields. Normal prompt assembly renders those fields through `buildInboundUserContextPrefix(...)`, so `BodyForAgent` must stay clean to avoid duplicate plaintext attribution in `BodyStripped`.

A self-forward keeps the same typed attribution too: even when `ForwardedFromId` equals `SenderId`, the message is still forwarded context, not a fresh direct instruction to the agent.

When multiple forwarded Telegram updates arrive together, the forward burst debounce path now builds one model-visible turn with clean joined body text plus a typed `UntrustedStructuredContext` entry for each forwarded item. The synthetic message strips inherited Telegram forward fields so the batch is not represented as if every item came from only the first origin.

For mixed media/text bursts, `body_line` is assigned only to entries that actually contribute non-empty text to the joined `BodyForAgent`; media-only forwarded entries keep `item_index`/`message_id` without a misleading line reference.

## Scenario coverage

- Direct forward: clean `BodyForAgent`, typed `ForwardedFrom*`, prompt-visible `Forwarded message context` block.
- Self-forward: `SenderId == ForwardedFromId` still keeps typed forwarded context.
- Burst/batch forward: one turn, clean joined body, per-item prompt-visible `Telegram forwarded batch item` metadata.
- Mixed text + media-only forward burst: media-only item does not claim a nonexistent `body_line`.

## Review feedback addressed

- Addressed ClawSweeper P2: `BodyForAgent` remains clean; forwarded context is carried as typed metadata / structured context.
- Addressed ClawSweeper P2: forwarded batch `body_line` now tracks actual joined body lines and is omitted for media-only entries.

## Test Plan

- `corepack pnpm exec vitest run extensions/telegram/src/bot.create-telegram-bot.test.ts extensions/telegram/src/bot.test.ts extensions/telegram/src/bot-message-context.forwarded.test.ts extensions/telegram/src/bot.media.downloads-media-file-path-no-file-download.e2e.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/inbound-meta.test.ts`
- `git diff --check`

target.title: fix(usage-cost): surface unpriced-model spend as missingCostEntries instead of a confident $0
target.body:
## Summary

- **Problem:** `openclaw gateway usage-cost` reports `totalCost: 0` with `missingCostEntries: 0` for models that ship an all-zero `cost` block (e.g. codex `gpt-5.5`, whose Codex backend exposes no per-token price). Real pay-per-token usage is therefore reported as a confident, *complete* `$0`, and every budget / spike / daily-spend safeguard that keys off `totalCost` is silently blind to it.
- **Solution:** In `scanTranscriptFile`, when a model's resolved pricing is *unknown* — no positive per-token rate and no tiered pricing — and the turn actually burned tokens, drop the transport's fabricated `$0` and surface the turn as a missing-cost entry instead of recording a confident `$0`.
- **What changed:** `src/infra/session-cost-usage.ts` — new `isModelPricingKnown()` helper and a sibling branch in `scanTranscriptFile` that mirrors the existing tiered-pricing override. `src/infra/session-cost-usage.test.ts` — regression test.
- **What did NOT change (scope boundary):** Models with positive or tiered pricing are unaffected; entries with zero token usage are not flagged; a transport-recorded *positive* cost is preserved; recorded costs for priced models are untouched. No change to model-catalog generation or the gateway pricing cache.

## Motivation

A freshly-onboarded, idle host on `2026.5.20` (default `openai/gpt-5.5`, `api_key` auth) accrued ~1.78M tokens/day, yet `usage-cost` reported `$0` with `missingCostEntries: 0`, so budget/spike monitoring never fired. Surfacing unknown-priced usage as *missing* lets downstream tooling tell "genuinely free" apart from "price unknown" instead of trusting a false `$0`. Tracks #85858.

## Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix
- [ ] Feature
- [ ] Refactor required for the fix
- [ ] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [x] Gateway / orchestration
- [ ] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [ ] Memory / storage
- [ ] Integrations
- [ ] API / contracts
- [ ] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Related #85858
- [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior or issue addressed: `usage-cost` reports a confident `$0` (`missingCostEntries: 0`) for unpriced `gpt-5.5` usage, hiding real spend from budget/spike monitoring.
- Real environment tested: self-hosted OpenClaw `2026.5.20 (e510042)` on Linux; default model `openai/gpt-5.5`; `api_key` auth.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: applied the equivalent change to the installed build, restarted the gateway, then ran `openclaw gateway usage-cost --days 2 --json --expect-final` over the same already-logged usage.
- Evidence after fix: copied live terminal output of the `openclaw gateway usage-cost` command — before vs. after the patch, same logs:

```
BEFORE (shipped build) -- openclaw gateway usage-cost --days 2 --json
  totals: { totalTokens: 1780235, totalCost: 0, missingCostEntries: 0 }

AFTER (patched build) -- same command, same already-logged usage
  totals: { totalTokens: 1780235, totalCost: 0, missingCostEntries: 32 }
```

- Observed result after fix: the same 1,780,235 tokens of otherwise-invisible unpriced usage are now surfaced as 32 missing-cost entries; `totalCost` stays `0` (no fabricated price is invented).
- What was not tested: models configured with *intentional* zero pricing (these would also be surfaced as missing — see scope note below); the macOS/Control-UI cost views.
- Before evidence: included in the terminal capture above.

## Root Cause (if applicable)

- **Root cause:** codex models ship `cost: {input:0, output:0, cacheRead:0, cacheWrite:0}` in the generated `models.json` because the Codex backend exposes no per-token price. `resolveModelCostConfig` returns that *defined* zero, `estimateUsageCost` computes a confident `0`, and the transport records `cost.total: 0` per turn. Cost aggregation (`applyCostTotal` / `applyCostBreakdown`) only treats `undefined` — not `0` — as missing, so "price unknown" is indistinguishable from "genuinely free."

## Note on the free-vs-unknown distinction

This fix treats an all-zero resolved cost as "unknown" only when the turn has no trustworthy recorded cost (recorded cost is `0`/absent), so a turn that carries a real positive cost is always preserved. A model configured with an *intentional* `0` price would still be surfaced as missing; in practice all-zero catalog costs mean "we don't have a price." A future refinement could add an explicit "free" marker (e.g. `cost: null` vs `cost: 0`) so the two can be told apart; this PR keeps to the minimal, provider-agnostic change that stops the silent `$0`.

## Summary

- document raw regex-body syntax and regenerate config/help metadata

## Notes
- Recreated from a clean `origin/main` branch after #70846 was closed as dirty; this branch has been rebased on current `origin/main` and generated baselines refreshed after upstream movement.

## Validation
- `pnpm --config.minimum-release-age=0 install --lockfile-only --ignore-scripts=false --config.engine-strict=false --config.enable-pre-post-scripts=true --config.side-effects-cache=true`
- `pnpm plugin-sdk:api:check`
- `pnpm config:channels:check`
- `pnpm test extensions/qa-channel/src/inbound.test.ts src/channels/mention-pattern-policy.test.ts extensions/telegram/src/bot-message-context.body.test.ts`

- `git diff --check`

## Real behavior proof
- **Behavior or issue addressed:** Scoped mention-pattern policy now resolves the effective provider/agent/conversation policy so configured regex mentions are enabled only for explicitly allowed conversations in deny mode and remain disabled for denied or unlisted conversations. Latest follow-up rebased onto current `origin/main`, resolved refreshed generated config/plugin SDK baseline conflicts, and reran the exact-head behavior proof.
- **Real environment tested:** Local OpenClaw PR checkout on host `patrick`, branch `feature/mention-pattern-policy-clean`, head `c4a2be36ad7dd3a6d544a4dab693efb978f18494`, running the real source resolver, Telegram group peer id helper, mention matcher, and inbound mention decision code through Node/tsx against redacted OpenClaw-style config/message facts. This does not exercise live external delivery.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch:** `cd /home/patrick/projects/openclaw-mention-pattern-policy && HEAD_SHA=c4a2be36ad7dd3a6d544a4dab693efb978f18494 HEAD_SHORT=c4a2be36ad node --import tsx /tmp/pr70864-proof-02d093.mjs`
- **Evidence after fix:** Terminal output from the real source smoke:

```text
OpenClaw scoped mention policy Telegram account media pre-download proof (c4a2be36ad, c4a2be36ad7dd3a6d544a4dab693efb978f18494)
{"topic":7,"conversationId":"-1001234567893:topic:7","accountPolicyAllowIn":["-1001234567893:topic:7"],"globalProviderAllowIn":["-1001234567893:topic:global-only"],"mentionPatternsEnabled":true,"wasMentioned":true,"shouldSkip":false,"skipReason":null}
{"topic":9,"conversationId":"-1001234567893:topic:9","accountPolicyAllowIn":["-1001234567893:topic:7"],"globalProviderAllowIn":["-1001234567893:topic:global-only"],"mentionPatternsEnabled":false,"wasMentioned":false,"shouldSkip":true,"skipReason":null}
{"topic":11,"conversationId":"-1001234567893:topic:11","accountPolicyAllowIn":["-1001234567893:topic:7"],"globalProviderAllowIn":["-1001234567893:topic:global-only"],"mentionPatternsEnabled":false,"wasMentioned":false,"shouldSkip":true,"skipReason":null}
```

- **Observed result after fix:** In inherited `deny` mode, the media pre-download path honors the Telegram account policy rather than the global provider fallback: account-allowed topic `-1001234567893:topic:7` enables configured regex mention `bot` and media pre-download is not skipped, while account-denied topic `:topic:9` and unlisted topic `:topic:11` disable configured regex mentions and skip unaddressed group media before download.

target.title: telegram: allow last blockquote to be expandable
target.body:
## Summary - Adds Telegram formatter support for Telegram's native expandable blockquote HTML form. - Preserves existing normal blockquote rendering. - Adds formatter coverage for expandable blockquotes. ## Changes - Render expandable blockquote metadata as `
`. - Preserve existing blockquote rendering behavior. - Add formatter coverage for expandable blockquotes. - Problem: OpenClaw's Telegram formatter could not represent Telegram's native expandable blockquote HTML form. - Solution: Add explicit formatter support for expandable blockquotes. - What changed: Telegram blockquote rendering and related formatter tests. - What did NOT change (scope boundary): Gateway orchestration, runtime delivery, auth, model routing, and default channel behavior. ## Motivation Telegram supports expandable blockquotes natively. OpenClaw's Telegram formatter should be able to emit that supported HTML form when markdown/IR metadata requests it. ## Change Type (select all) - [x] Bug fix - [ ] Feature - [ ] Refactor required for the fix - [ ] Docs - [ ] Security hardening - [ ] Chore/infra ## Scope (select all touched areas) - [ ] Gateway / orchestration - [ ] Skills / tool execution - [ ] Auth / tokens - [ ] Memory / storage - [x] Integrations - [ ] API / contracts - [ ] UI / DX - [ ] CI/CD / infra ## Linked Issue/PR - Closes # - Related #84082 - [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression ## Coordination with #84082 Related PR #84082 handles the raw Telegram HTML sanitizer path for user-supplied `
` markup. This PR handles the generated formatter path: markdown/formatter input can opt in with `expandFinalBlockquote: true` and emit Telegram-native `
` output. These paths are complementary and can land separately. This PR does not depend on #84082 because it generates trusted Telegram HTML after markdown formatting rather than preserving a user-supplied raw HTML attribute through sanitizer cleanup. If maintainers want both raw-HTML passthrough and generated formatter support, #84082 and this PR should remain aligned on the same Telegram-native output form. ## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs) - Behavior or issue addressed: Telegram expandable blockquote formatting was verified in a real OpenClaw setup using the Telegram integration. - Real environment tested: OpenClaw 2026.5.18 installed runtime on macOS with Telegram channel enabled. - Exact steps or command run after this patch: Ran the installed-runtime formatter command below, then observed the rendered message in Telegram in collapsed and expanded states. - Evidence after fix: Live formatter command output plus Telegram screenshots: - Collapsed screenshot: https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/b590a21f-5b43-4bea-b917-9bba48fa42e2 - Expanded screenshot: https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/77508c27-954a-40bb-a0ae-e4666a08d95e - Console output: ```html
Usage: 1 in / 1 out · cache 0/0 · est $0.01 GPT-5.5: expandable signature verification Grok: none Ollama-14B: none session agent:main:telegram:direct:7456384535
``` - Observed result after fix: Telegram displayed the trailing blockquote collapsed initially and expanded it in-place when tapped. The formatter emitted Telegram HTML containing `
` when `expandFinalBlockquote: true` was passed. - What was not tested: Other Telegram clients beyond the tested client. Formatter command: ```sh node --input-type=module <<'EOF' const mod = await import('file:///opt/homebrew/lib/node_modules/openclaw/dist/format-AZK6G0is.js'); const sample = [ 'DM expandable signature test', '', '> Usage: 1 in / 1 out · cache 0/0 · est $0.01', '> GPT-5.5: expandable signature verification', '> Grok: none', '> Ollama-14B: none', '> session agent:main:telegram:direct:7456384535' ].join('\n'); console.log(mod.a(sample, { textMode: 'markdown', expandFinalBlockquote: true })); EOF ``` Formatter output: ```html
Usage: 1 in / 1 out · cache 0/0 · est $0.01 GPT-5.5: expandable signature verification Grok: none Ollama-14B: none session agent:main:telegram:direct:7456384535
``` ## Verification - Ran the Telegram formatter tests. - Verified live Telegram rendering in a real OpenClaw installation. - Behavior or issue addressed: Telegram formatter can now emit native expandable blockquote HTML. - Real environment tested: OpenClaw 2026.5.18 installed runtime on macOS with Telegram channel enabled. - Exact steps or command run after this patch: Ran the live installed-runtime formatter check above, then verified the rendered result in Telegram with collapsed and expanded screenshots. - Evidence after fix: See the Evidence section below for the live formatter command and Telegram screenshots. - Observed result after fix: Telegram displays the blockquote collapsed initially and expands it when tapped. - What was not tested: Other Telegram clients beyond the tested client. - Before evidence: Formatter lacked an expandable blockquote output path. ## Root Cause (if applicable) - Root cause: The Telegram formatter only emitted normal blockquote HTML and did not carry expandable blockquote metadata through to Telegram's native `
` form. - Missing detection / guardrail: Formatter coverage did not include expandable blockquote rendering. - Contributing context (if known): N/A ## Regression Test Plan (if applicable) - Coverage level that should have caught this: - [x] Unit test - [ ] Seam / integration test - [ ] End-to-end test - [ ] Existing coverage already sufficient - Target test or file: Telegram formatter tests. - Scenario the test should lock in: Expandable blockquote metadata renders as `
`, while normal blockquotes continue rendering normally. - Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail: The behavior is a deterministic formatter output decision. - Existing test that already covers this (if any): Existing blockquote formatter coverage covered normal blockquotes. - If no new test is added, why not: N/A ## User-visible / Behavior Changes OpenClaw can now render Telegram expandable blockquotes when requested by the formatter input. ## Diagram (if applicable) N/A ## Security Impact (required) - New permissions/capabilities? (`No`) - Secrets/tokens handling changed? (`No`) - New/changed network calls? (`No`) - Command/tool execution surface changed? (`No`) - Data access scope changed? (`No`) - If any `Yes`, explain risk + mitigation: N/A ## Repro + Verification ### Environment - OS: macOS - Runtime/container: local OpenClaw install - Model/provider: N/A - Integration/channel (if any): Telegram - Relevant config (redacted): Telegram channel enabled ### Steps 1. Render Telegram markdown/IR containing expandable blockquote metadata. 2. Send or observe the rendered output in Telegram. 3. Expand the blockquote in Telegram. ### Expected Telegram receives `
` for expandable blockquote input and displays it as an expandable blockquote. ### Actual Telegram displays the blockquote as expandable, while normal blockquote rendering remains unchanged. ## Evidence - [ ] Failing test/log before + passing after - [ ] Trace/log snippets - [x] Screenshot/recording - [ ] Perf numbers (if relevant) Collapsed: Expanded: ## Human Verification (required) What you personally verified (not just CI), and how: - Verified scenarios: Formatter test coverage and live Telegram expandable blockquote rendering. - Edge cases checked: Normal blockquote rendering remains normal. - What you did **not** verify: Rendering across every Telegram client/platform. ## Review Conversations - [ ] I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR. - [ ] I left unresolved only the conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment. If a bot review conversation is addressed by this PR, resolve that conversation yourself. Do not leave bot review conversation cleanup for maintainers. ## Compatibility / Migration - Backward compatible? (`Yes`) - Config/env changes? (`No`) - Migration needed? (`No`) - If yes, exact upgrade steps: N/A ## Risks and Mitigations - Risk: Telegram HTML support may vary slightly across Telegram clients. - Mitigation: The change uses Telegram's native expandable blockquote HTML form and keeps existing normal blockquote behavior unchanged.
